atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: The applicant's age in years at filing.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: The applicant's years as a lawful permanent resident at filing.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 4
atoms.statement: Months physically present in the United States during the five-year statutory period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 4
atoms.statement: Months physically present in the United States during the three-year spouse statutory period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: Months residing in the state or USCIS district before filing.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: Days absent from the United States for the first material trip in the statutory period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: Days absent from the United States for the second material trip in the statutory period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: Days absent from the United States for the third material trip in the statutory period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: Days absent from the United States for the fourth material trip in the statutory period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: Aggregate days imprisoned during the statutory period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: Months married to the U.S. citizen spouse.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: Months living in marital union with the U.S. citizen spouse.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: Months since return from the last potentially residence-breaking absence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: The applicant was an LPR at filing.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: The applicant remains an LPR at examination.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: The application was signed by the applicant or a legally permitted signer.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: The applicant's identity is established.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: Biometric requirements are completed or waived.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: There is no final order of removal that defeats eligibility.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: Removal proceedings do not bar adjudication or an exception applies.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: Selective Service registration is satisfied or the applicant is exempt.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: Tax filing history is compliant or adequately explained.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: Child support obligations are compliant or adequately explained.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: Required documents for the selected eligibility track are submitted.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: Material application questions are answered.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: The filing jurisdiction matches the applicant's residence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: There is no pending proceeding that independently prevents approval.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 1
atoms.statement: The record is sufficiently reviewable for a benchmark determination.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: The applicant seeks naturalization under the general five-year track.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: The applicant seeks naturalization under the three-year U.S. citizen spouse track.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: The spouse has been a U.S. citizen for the required three-year period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: The spouse remains a U.S. citizen through the relevant period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: The applicant has lived in marital union with the U.S. citizen spouse for the required period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: The applicant has been living with the U.S. citizen spouse for the required period.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: Spouse-track documents are submitted.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: No separation disrupts the spouse-track marital union.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: No divorce or annulment defeats the spouse track.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 2-4
atoms.statement: Residence facts are consistent with the spouse track.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: No absence from the United States exceeded one year.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: If a six-to-twelve-month absence occurred, evidence rebuts abandonment of residence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: The applicant maintained a U.S. home during relevant absences.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: The applicant maintained family ties in the United States during relevant absences.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: The applicant maintained U.S. employment or did not take foreign employment inconsistent with residence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: The applicant did not claim nonresident status for tax purposes.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: The applicant returned to the United States after relevant absences.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: Travel records are complete enough to assess continuous residence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: Passport or travel records support the stated travel history.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: There are no unexplained gaps in the travel history.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: No absence breaks continuous residence for this benchmark.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: Residence was reestablished for the required period after any breaking absence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: A qualifying employment abroad exception applies if needed.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: An N-470 preservation approval applies if needed.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: A religious vocation abroad exception applies if needed.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: A military service residence exception applies if needed.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: The applicant had an absence of more than six months but less than one year.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 3
atoms.statement: The applicant had an absence of one year or more.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 4
atoms.statement: Physical presence calculations are reliable.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 4
atoms.statement: Entry and exit records are consistent.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 4
atoms.statement: No unresolved gap affects physical presence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 4
atoms.statement: The applicable statutory period is selected correctly.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part D, Ch. 4
atoms.statement: Any early filing window is applied correctly.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: The applicant passed the English test.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: The applicant satisfied the English reading requirement.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: The applicant satisfied the English writing requirement.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: The applicant satisfied the English speaking requirement.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: The applicant passed the civics test.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: Simplified civics treatment was administered when applicable.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: Required retest opportunities were completed when applicable.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: Reexamination was passed or an exception applies.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: A medical disability exception to English/civics requirements is approved.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: The applicant qualifies for interpreter support where relevant.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part E
atoms.statement: The applicant understands the oath language or an exception applies.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: The applicant is willing to take the Oath of Allegiance.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: The applicant is attached to the principles of the Constitution.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: The applicant is well disposed to the good order and happiness of the United States.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: The applicant accepts required oath-related service commitments or a permitted modification.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: Communist party membership does not bar eligibility or an exception applies.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: Totalitarian party membership does not bar eligibility or an exception applies.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: The applicant does not advocate overthrow of the U.S. government.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: The applicant has no disqualifying desertion issue.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: No title of nobility issue defeats the oath.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part J
atoms.statement: Oath-related answers are truthful.

binding_policy.notes: A false murder-conviction binding requires a closed-world criminal-history or court-clearance source.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has a murder conviction.

binding_policy.notes: A false aggravated-felony bar binding requires a closed-world criminal-history or court-clearance source, not narrative silence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has an aggravated felony conviction after November 29, 1990.

atoms.statement: The applicant participated in persecution.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant participated in genocide.

atoms.statement: The applicant participated in torture.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant participated in extrajudicial killing.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has a controlled-substance violation other than a single offense involving 30 grams or less of marijuana.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant admitted conduct constituting a drug offense.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant engaged in drug trafficking.

atoms.statement: The applicant is a habitual drunkard during the statutory period.

atoms.statement: The applicant's principal income came from illegal gambling.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has multiple gambling convictions.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant engaged in prostitution or commercialized vice.

atoms.statement: The applicant engaged in alien smuggling without an applicable exception.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant practiced polygamy.

binding_policy.notes: A false false-testimony binding requires an immigration/interview record with relevant coverage.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant gave false testimony to obtain an immigration benefit.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant willfully failed to support dependents.

atoms.statement: The applicant committed adultery that tended to destroy a marriage.

atoms.statement: The applicant unlawfully voted.

atoms.statement: The applicant made a false claim to U.S. citizenship.

atoms.statement: The applicant willfully failed to register for Selective Service when required.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant willfully failed to comply with tax obligations.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant engaged in fraud or deceit.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant engaged in identity or document fraud.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant committed domestic violence.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant committed child abuse.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant violated a protective order.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant committed unlawful harassment or stalking.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant committed manslaughter or another serious violent crime.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has a DUI pattern or reckless-endangerment conduct relevant to GMC.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has unpaid restitution or unresolved court orders.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has uncompleted probation or parole.

binding_policy.notes: A false pending-charge binding requires a closed-world criminal-history or docket search source.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has a pending criminal charge.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: An arrest disposition is unresolved.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant made a material misrepresentation to obtain a benefit.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant unlawfully registered to vote.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: A removal order or proceeding after filing affects eligibility.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant engaged in terrorism-related activity.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has Nazi-persecution-related inadmissibility conduct.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant has a serious immigration violation relevant to GMC.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant entered a sham marriage for an immigration benefit.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant smuggled a family member without an applicable exception.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant failed to pay required taxes.

atoms.statement: The applicant committed Social Security fraud.

atoms.source_span: USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12, Part F
atoms.statement: The applicant committed public-benefits fraud.

atoms.statement: The applicant was confined to a penal institution for 180 days or more during the statutory period.

atoms.statement: The travel pattern is complex enough to require human review.

atoms.source_span: RuleKit benchmark governance trigger
atoms.statement: Travel history is internally inconsistent.

atoms.statement: Critical dates are missing from the evidence.

atoms.statement: A tax issue remains unresolved.

atoms.source_span: RuleKit benchmark governance trigger
atoms.statement: Selective Service facts are disputed.

atoms.source_span: RuleKit benchmark governance trigger
atoms.statement: The record suggests possible false testimony requiring review.

atoms.source_span: RuleKit benchmark governance trigger
atoms.statement: The record suggests a discretionary GMC issue requiring review.

atoms.source_span: RuleKit benchmark governance trigger
atoms.statement: Accommodation or disability documentation is unclear.

atoms.source_span: RuleKit benchmark governance trigger
atoms.statement: Identity or name records conflict.

atoms.statement: Interpreter or understanding facts require human review.

atoms.source_span: RuleKit benchmark governance trigger
atoms.statement: A claimed legal exception requires human review.

atoms.source_span: RuleKit benchmark governance trigger
atoms.statement: The case contains facts outside the benchmark's encoded scope.

USCIS Policy Manual, Vol. 12 selected naturalization requirements
nodes.surface_label: selected N-400 benchmark requirements satisfied without escalation

policy_text: Selected N-400 naturalization eligibility benchmark. The applicant must satisfy core threshold requirements, an applicable residence track, continuous residence, physical presence, state or USCIS district residence, good moral character, English and civics requirements or accepted exceptions, and oath/attachment requirements. Certain missing, inconsistent, pending, or complex facts require human review. Numeric requirements are represented with typed arithmetic and comparison nodes where possible.
version_label: bounded benchmark: USCIS Policy Manual Volume 12 selected concepts
